label: End-to-End Engineering
description: Composite benchmark extending the default profile with architecture, complex coding, and hygiene tasks. Tests whether a skill library supports the full engineering lifecycle — from planning through clean implementation to maintainable output.

tasks.prompt_text: Review the library and determine whether its skills help agents produce architecturally sound output — clear layering, correct dependency direction, and explicit ownership boundaries in generated code or plans.
tasks.rubric_text:
0 no architectural awareness
1 flat structure with no layering
2 some organization but unclear dependencies
3 clear architectural output with layering and dependency direction
4 strong architectural quality with enforced contracts, ownership clarity, and composition patterns

tasks.prompt_text: Review the library and determine whether its skills help agents make sound build-vs-buy and abstraction-level decisions — when to introduce a new module, when to inline, and when to use an existing component.
tasks.rubric_text:
0 no abstraction-level guidance
1 vague modularity mention
2 some decision awareness
3 clear build-vs-buy criteria with abstraction-level heuristics
4 strong decision framework with cost analysis, reuse criteria, and explicit inline-vs-extract triggers

tasks.prompt_text: Review the library and determine whether its skills help agents produce implementation that handles errors, edge cases, and unexpected inputs systematically rather than only the happy path.
tasks.rubric_text:
0 happy path only
1 vague error mention
2 some error handling but gaps in edge coverage
3 clear error and edge case handling with systematic approach
4 strong implementation rigor with error taxonomy, edge case enumeration, and defensive contracts

tasks.prompt_text: Review the library and determine whether its skills help agents write code that is testable by design — injectable dependencies, pure functions where possible, and clear interfaces for mocking.
tasks.rubric_text:
0 no testability awareness
1 vague testing mention
2 some testability but tightly coupled
3 clear testable design with dependency injection and mockable interfaces
4 strong testability discipline with pure function preference, explicit seams, and test-friendly architecture

tasks.prompt_text: Review the library and determine whether its skills help agents maintain code hygiene throughout a multi-step task — keeping diffs clean, removing dead code as it appears, and not leaving TODO debris.
tasks.rubric_text:
0 no hygiene maintenance
1 cleanup as afterthought
2 some hygiene awareness during implementation
3 clear continuous hygiene with clean-as-you-go discipline
4 strong hygiene throughout with diff minimization, immediate dead-code removal, and zero-TODO-debt policy

tasks.prompt_text: Review the library and determine whether its skills help agents produce output that a human reviewer can approve quickly — readable, well-structured, minimal, and self-documenting.
tasks.rubric_text:
0 unreadable output
1 functional but hard to review
2 some readability effort
3 clear reviewer-friendly output with good structure and naming
4 strong review-ready code with self-documenting patterns, minimal cognitive load, and obvious intent
